title: The Key in the Drawer
description: After moving into a new apartment, Lucas finds a mysterious key hidden in a kitchen drawer. At first it seems meaningless, but a strange discovery in the apartment suggests that the key might open something far more unexpected. 🕵️‍♂️

words.definition_en: Having a low temperature.
words.example_en: A cold wind whistled through the trees.

words.definition_en: Chemical elements or alloys, their ores, and the mines where their ores come from.
words.example_en: Manganism has been known about since the 19th century, when miners exposed to ores containing manganese, a silvery metal, began to totter, slur their speech and behave like someone inebriated.

words.definition_en: Expressing distance or motion.
words.example_en: Take the chicken out of the freezer.

words.definition_en: Used before a noun phrase, including a simple noun
words.example_en: I’m reading the book Mary reviewed. (Compare I’m reading a book Mary reviewed.)

words.definition_en: An object designed to open and close a lock.
words.example_en: We tiptoed into the house, up the stairs and along the hall into the room where the Professor had been spending so much of his time. 'Twas locked, of course, but the Deacon man got a big bunch of keys out of his pocket and commenced to putter with the lock.

questions.question: Where did Lucas find the mysterious key?
options: Under the bed in the bedroom
At the back of a kitchen drawer

questions.question: Why did the key seem unusual to Lucas?
options: It had a label attached to it
It looked old but carefully polished

questions.question: What did Lucas try to do after finding the key?
options: Use it to open different locks in the apartment

questions.question: Arrange the words to form a correct sentence from the story.
